introSection: Nitro is a steel hyper roller coaster located at Six Flags Great Adventure in Jackson, New Jersey, USA. Opened on April 7, 2001, it was the third Bolliger & Mabillard Hyper Coaster installation after Apollo's Chariot at Busch Gardens Williamsburg and Raging Bull at Six Flags Great America. The ride is tall, has a  tall drop and a top speed of "nearly ".

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Construction of a new roller coaster began in August 2000. Rumored names for the attraction included "Superman Krypton Coaster", even though the colors of the ride weren't blue and red like the other Superman rides. The ride's structure began to rise in December 2000.

The queue line was built where Fantasy Fling stood previously. Fantasy Fling was moved to be near Buccaneer.

The ride's name Nitro was officially announced on February 1, 2001.

Nitro opened on April 7, 2001. An opening ceremony was held on April 11, with former WWE wrestler Mankind (Mick Foley).

At the time of its opening, it was the fastest roller coaster in New Jersey and the tallest roller coaster on the East Coast. It has the third steepest drop from a lift hill in the park, behind El Toro with a 76° drop, and the Jersey Devil Coaster with an 87° drop. Nitro's lift hill once featured signs on the stairways comparing its height to landmarks such as Niagra Falls, however these have since been removed.

A single rider entrance was added for the 2022 season.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Nitro is tall and its tallest drop is, at a 66-degree angle. On a clear night, the Philadelphia skyline (which is 55 miles away) is visible from the top of the ride. The lift hill and final brake run of the coaster pass the water reservoir for the nearby Roaring Rapids.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Yellow track with pink rails and blue supports.

fullText:
Nitro is a steel hyper roller coaster located at Six Flags Great Adventure in Jackson, New Jersey, USA. Opened on April 7, 2001, it was the third Bolliger & Mabillard Hyper Coaster installation after Apollo's Chariot at Busch Gardens Williamsburg and Raging Bull at Six Flags Great America. The ride is tall, has a  tall drop and a top speed of "nearly ".

Construction of a new roller coaster began in August 2000. Rumored names for the attraction included "Superman Krypton Coaster", even though the colors of the ride weren't blue and red like the other Superman rides. The ride's structure began to rise in December 2000.

The queue line was built where Fantasy Fling stood previously. Fantasy Fling was moved to be near Buccaneer.

The ride's name Nitro was officially announced on February 1, 2001.

Nitro opened on April 7, 2001. An opening ceremony was held on April 11, with former WWE wrestler Mankind (Mick Foley).

At the time of its opening, it was the fastest roller coaster in New Jersey and the tallest roller coaster on the East Coast. It has the third steepest drop from a lift hill in the park, behind El Toro with a 76° drop, and the Jersey Devil Coaster with an 87° drop. Nitro's lift hill once featured signs on the stairways comparing its height to landmarks such as Niagra Falls, however these have since been removed.

A single rider entrance was added for the 2022 season.

Nitro is tall and its tallest drop is, at a 66-degree angle. On a clear night, the Philadelphia skyline (which is 55 miles away) is visible from the top of the ride. The lift hill and final brake run of the coaster pass the water reservoir for the nearby Roaring Rapids.

Yellow track with pink rails and blue supports.
